commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d07618911e1383ed0298f56887d3ad701004d7da)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes native command spec generation so `nativeAliases` remain lookup aliases but are no longer emitted as separate native command menu specs.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main expands `nativeAliases` into native command specs, and Telegram feeds those specs into a 100-command capped menu payload. I did not run live Telegram proof in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer review because the current repair crosses shared native command/plugin SDK behavior and the external PR still lacks real Telegram proof.

target.title: fix(telegram): keep native aliases out of command menus
target.body:
## Summary
- Stop expanding chat command nativeAliases into native command menu specs.
- Keep native alias lookup intact so /side still resolves to the /btw command without consuming a Telegram menu slot.

Fixes #85199.

## Verification
Behavior addressed: Telegram native command registration advertises canonical native command names only; nativeAliases continue to route inbound command lookup.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source worktree on Linux Node 22.22.2.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/commands-registry.test.ts
Evidence after fix: commands-registry.test.ts passed, 28 tests.
Observed result after fix: /side remains accepted by findCommandByNativeName and text alias normalization, but listNativeCommandSpecs no longer includes side.
What was not tested: live Telegram Bot API setMyCommands registration.
target.author: clawsean
